private.name: Apex Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.3B

data.narrative:
Axelspace is a Series D-stage space and aerospace company founded by Yuya Nakamura. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 private peers in the same sector at similar or adjacent funding stages: Sierra Space ($8.0B, Series C), Apex Space ($2.3B, Series D), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B, Series C), and Skyryse ($1.1B, Series C). A broader reference set includes 5 public Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 additional private peers.

Peer-median analysis is the applicable valuation method. The median valuation across the 4-company peer set is $1.9B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $8.0B. The wide dispersion reflects stage variation and heterogeneous business models within the sector. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 25.8x across 6 public tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data, typical for private companies at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range supported by this analysis is $1.6B to $2.2B, centered on $1.9B. This median reflects Axelspace's positioning within its peer cohort. Key uncertainties include business model specificity within space infrastructure, near-term capital requirements and dilution, and revenue visibility relative to comparable firms. The wide observed peer range ($1.1B–$8.0B) suggests material sensitivity to company-specific operational metrics and market position not fully captured in this stage-based comparison.
